title: A Neural Algorithm of Artistic Style
authors: Leon A. Gatys, Alexander S. Ecker, Matthias Bethge
hook: See a photo painted in the style of Van Gogh or Picasso, instantly.
promise: You will understand how neural networks can separate and remix the content and style of images.
prerequisite_note: Bring your curiosity about how computers see images, no deep math or coding needed.

glossary.plain: A type of neural network especially good at finding patterns in images.
The high-level information in an image, like objects and their arrangement.
The textures, colors, and brushstrokes that give an image its artistic feel.
A way to capture how features in an image relate to each other, used to represent style.
Automatically changing an image until it matches the desired content and style.
A grid of numbers showing where certain patterns appear in the image.

cards.title: Paint Like a Neural Net
body: See how AI blends the look of one image with the structure of another.

cards.title: What if computers could paint?
body: Imagine taking a photo of your cat and making it look like a Van Gogh painting. Gatys and colleagues show how a neural network can do just that.
By teasing apart what an image shows from how it looks, the authors unlocked a new way to create art using AI.

cards.title: From photograph to masterpiece
body: First, a neural network analyzes both a content image and a style image, breaking them down into patterns.
It then creates a new image, gradually adjusting it so its content matches the first image and its style matches the second.
The result is a picture with the subject of your photo but the brushwork and color of your chosen artist.
cards.caption: The process turns two images into a unique artistic blend.

cards.title: Before and after neural style transfer
body: See how traditional filters differ from neural style transfer.
cards.offNote: Only simple effects like blur or color shift, no real style mixing.
cards.onNote: Captures complex textures and patterns, creating true artistic blends.

cards.title: Separating what and how
body: The neural network learns to tell apart the 'what' (the objects and their shapes) from the 'how' (the textures and colors) in an image.
By optimizing a new image to match the content of one and the style of another, it creates a seamless artistic fusion.

cards.title: Balance content and style
body: Slide to see how changing the balance affects the final image.
cards.low: The image looks like the original photo with light artistic touches.
cards.mid: A balanced mix, both recognizable subject and clear artistic style.
cards.high: The image is heavily stylized, sometimes losing the original subject.

cards.title: Choose your neural palette
body: Pick the ingredients needed for neural style transfer.

choices.label: Content image
choices.note: The photo or subject you want to keep.

choices.label: Style image
choices.note: The artwork whose look you want to borrow.

choices.label: Pretrained CNN
choices.note: A network trained to see image patterns.

choices.label: Content loss function
choices.note: Measures how much the new image matches the content.

choices.label: Style loss function
choices.note: Measures how much the new image matches the style.

choices.label: Image optimizer
choices.note: Tweaks the new image to bring content and style together.

cards.title: AI as an artist
body: Neural style transfer became a viral sensation, inspiring artists, apps, and new research in AI creativity.
It proved that neural networks can learn abstract concepts like style, not just recognize objects.

bars.label: Flexibility
bars.note: Works with any combination of images

cards.caption: Neural style transfer made creative AI accessible to millions.

cards.title: Why this paper changed AI art
body: This work showed that neural networks can go beyond recognition, helping machines create and remix art in new ways.
It set the stage for a wave of AI-powered creativity, from photo filters to generative art and beyond.

cards.title: Neural Style Transfer in four moves

recap.label: Analyze
recap.note: Break images into content and style features.

recap.label: Separate
recap.note: Distinguish what is shown from how it looks.

recap.label: Blend
recap.note: Mix content from one image with style from another.

recap.label: Optimize
recap.note: Adjust the new image until both goals are met.

cards.title: A Neural Algorithm of Artistic Style
body: Read the original to see the math and experiments behind this creative leap.
